heading: CONVENTION MEASUREMENT
label: 143 CONVENTION MEASUREMENT
notes: Historical and Revision NotesChapter 143 provides implementing legislation for the International Convention on Tonnage Measurement of Ships (Convention) which came into effect in the United States on . Chapter 143, therefore, is based primarily on the Convention, not on provisions in existing U.S. law.February 10, 1983
Amendments2010—, , , substituted “Tonnage Certificate” for “International Tonnage Certificate (1969)” in item 14303.Pub. L. 111–281, title III, § 303(e)(3)124 Stat. 2925Oct. 15, 2010
